issue_title: Unable to load alignments for case 85
Session close timeout fails due to mixing relative and absolute times
issue_body:
The `sent_close_time` in `WebSocket.State` is an **absolute** epoch time (see [line 240](https://github.com/wildfoundry/dataplicity-lomond/blob/e37406c39d481ccb28dee43b221c0e2f6e595c44/lomond/websocket.py#L240)).  But `Session._check_close_timeout()` performs a comparison which is based on elapsed seconds **relative** to the start of the session (see [line 262](https://github.com/wildfoundry/dataplicity-lomond/blob/e37406c39d481ccb28dee43b221c0e2f6e595c44/lomond/session.py#L262)), thus the close timeout will never fire.  A simple fix would be to store "elapsed session time" in `sent_close_time`.

On a related note, it is a bit counter-intuitive to me that every call to `WebSocket.close()` updates the `sent_close_time`. Thus, for a case where multiple close packets happen to be sent, the close timeout check is always relative to the **last** packet rather than the **first**.  If one is relying on the close timeout check to close out a session where the server fails to respond, and there is a programming error where `close()` is being called periodically in a loop, then the timeout will likely never succeed.

issue_title: Flow with Vivado IP Integrator / External libraries
issue_body:
# Background
Recently I have been working with VUnit and Vivado IP Integrator (IPI) and running into library management difficulty. I have had success with using VUnit to map external libraries for Vivado IP and then simulating the IP - for example, simulating a single piece of IP (compiled into an external library) instantiated in a testbench (compiled into a VUnit managed library).

The primary difficulty is when plain HDL sources (that I would like to be managed by VUnit controlled library) are mixed into the Vivado IP Integrator block diagram.

In this configuration, IPI automatically creates a wrapper around the HDL file per-instance, and in the wrapper the HDL is instantiated by component instantiation with a default binding (no libraries, configurations, or entities are explicitly referenced). From my understanding of VHDL, the default binding implies it will search in the library that the component is compiled in, for an entity of the same name and port map as the component.

I bring this up because when initially planning on working with VUnit and Vivado IPI, I was hoping that I would be able to compile Vivado block diagram IP (e.g. all the generic Xilinx IP + project specific IP configurations) into a set of externally managed libraries, and then use VUnit to compile HDL sources into VUnit managed libraries. The typical flow would then be:

- Structure IPI block diagram in Vivado
- Run a (long running) process to generate IPI output products and compile Vivado IP for the block diagram
- Run VUnit simulations on portions of the block diagram, incrementally compiling changes to custom HDL files as development/debug continues.

My assumption has been I will reconfigure IP/block diagram infrequently, and will iterate on custom HDL designs frequently, so optimizing the compile/simulate loop for custom HDL is important.

Again, working with a single piece of IP at a time is straightforward since it can simply be instantiated by itself in a VUnit managed testbench. Working with a group of IP (in a block diagram hierarchy) or IP and HDL code is more difficult since portions of the block diagram itself must be instantiated - and have likely already been compiled together into various libraries as part of the Vivado IP compilation step.

I did investigate using VUnit to manage _all_ sources, but this is not feasible since some IP are encrypted and Vivado must be queried for proper compilation order and sources compiled separately (this is done in one of the examples in VUnit).

# Suggestions

- I believe this issue could be addressed, at least partially, by #179 - If VUnit supported a configuration as a top level simulation, then an explicit configuration could be used to bind the block diagram components for custom HDL entities explicitly to implementations in a VUnit managed library instead of the default binding that would occur to an entity in externally managed Vivado IP library. In this case, at least initially, the custom HDL source would be compiled identically into two different places: Vivado IP (external) library, and VUnit managed library for simulation only. After changes were made, the incremental compilation of VUnit managed library would get updated, and the version in the external library would not get updated or used.
- Allow VUnit to adopt/manage libraries that have already been created. I can imagine a lot of reasons why an approach like this could cause problems (e.g. with understanding dependencies). However, it seems like if a unit has already been successfully compiled into a library, there is no reason why VUnit could not take ownership of that library and simply recompile the file back into the same library (without wiping out the other contents).


If anyone has similar experiences or approaches that have worked for them, please let me know.

issue_title: es_repo_mgr cli is ignoring parent options for create sub-command
issue_body:
Either I'm doing something silly, or `click`/`es_repo_mgr` is not passing parent command command-line options to the `create` sub-command.

I was able to validate this by simply putting `print('connecting...', kwargs)` on [utils.py#L105](https://github.com/elastic/curator/blob/89e84f960b0188e6dd38dd48d66a4a67a4bb66dc/curator/cli/utils.py#L105).

Success with `show` command:
```
% es_repo_mgr --host foobar show                                                                                                                                             
('connecting...', {'url_prefix': u'', 'http_auth': None, 'loglevel': u'INFO', 'host': u'foobar', 'logformat': u'default', 'timeout': 30, 'debug': False, 'use_ssl': False, 'logfile': None, 'port': 9200})
```

Error with `create` command:

```
% es_repo_mgr --host foobar create fs --repository foo --location /tmp/foo
('connecting...', {})
```

I'm not familiar enough with the `click` library to tell if maybe someone just misconfigured it, but I know enough about python to be dangerous. Let me know if you need more info, or if I can help otherwise.

issue_title: [Profiles] Mechanism to select command interface depending on API profile
issue_body:
Unresolved issue from PR #2526.

We should investigate having a way of defining and easily getting method signatures for different api versions. Something like:
```Python
storage_account_create_sig = {
  'profile1': (resource_group_name, account_name, location, account_type, tags=None),
  'profile2': (resource_group_name, account_name, location, sku, kind, encryption=None, access_tier=None, tags=None)
}
```
Then you have a way of getting an API specific signature for reflecting on parameters and so forth and you can call into a common implementation method by treating those signatures as kwargs.

issue_title: az storage blob upload-batch outputs misleading error if --account-key is not attached
issue_body:
(I realize this may be obvious to most, especially as most other commands -h don't outline certain parameters as required even if they are but that doesn't make it a good practice and as a first time user of the CLI it can lead to some unnecessary trial and error, especially when the errors don't actually specify the correct error)

### Description
Basically, when running "az storage blob upload-batch -d https://[storageaccount].blob.core.windows.net/test-container -s C:\Users\<myuser>\storage-blob-integration-with-cdn-search-hdi\clinical-trials\clinical-
trials-small" 

it outputs "The specified resource does not exist."

This is misleading, as the resource does in-fact exist (I created it myself after-all) and it can see it and work with it. After some trial and error and reading through the -h I determined it's probably failing because the command is missing the account key, after adding --account-key and my information, the command is accepted and executes correctly, filling the container with the files. On a ubuntu 16.04 64Bit machine, I tried using the Ubuntu/Debian install, however a small problem emerged: apt refused the cli's repo because of some arch problems:
`N: Skipping acquire of configured file 'main/binary-i386/Packages' as repository 'https://apt-mo.trafficmanager.net/repos/azure-cli wheezy InRelease' doesn't support architecture 'i386'`

The fix was easy: just add `[arch=amd64]` to azure-cli's source.list.d line. I thought it useless to make a pull request for such a small fix, but you should modify the Debian/Ubuntu installation instructions to 

```
$ echo "deb [arch=amd64] https://apt-mo.trafficmanager.net/repos/azure-cli/ wheezy main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/azure-cli.list
$ sudo apt-key adv --keyserver apt-mo.trafficmanager.net --recv-keys 417A0893
$ sudo apt-get install apt-transport-https
$ sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install azure-cli
```

for 64Bit systems (which are, for this end of our year of 2016, fairly common ;) )

issue_title: Make TCPSite accept an already existing loop
issue_body: this makes it trivial to use aiohttp inside an existing larger app

## Long story short Currently, most of asyncio based web frameworks embeds http server into the web framework. If http/ws servers would use ASGI standard, then it would be possible to run your app using different http/ws servers. That is why I think it is important for aiohttp to add ASGI support. ## Expected behaviour I would like to write aiohttp app: ```python from aiohttp import web async def hello(request): return web.Response(text="Hello world!") app = web.Application() app.add_routes([web.get('/', hello)]) ``` And run it with any ASGI compatible server: ``` > daphne app:app ``` ``` > http -b get :8080/ Hello world! ``` ## Actual behaviour ``` > daphne app:app 2018-04-02 12:48:51,097 ERROR Traceback (most recent call last): File "daphne/http_protocol.py", line 158, in process "server": self.server_addr, File "daphne/server.py", line 184, in create_application application_instance = self.application(scope=scope) TypeError: __call__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'scope' 127.0.0.1:41828 - - [02/Apr/2018:12:48:51] "GET /" 500 452 ``` ``` > http -b get :8080/ 500 Internal Server Error

500 Internal Server Error

Daphne HTTP processing error

Daphne
``` ## ASGI resources https://github.com/django/asgiref/blob/master/specs/asgi.rst - ASGI specification. https://github.com/django/asgiref/blob/master/specs/www.rst - ASGI-HTTP and ASGI-WebSocket protocol specifications. Example ASGI app: ```python import json def app(scope): async def channel(receive, send): message = await receive() if scope['method'] == 'POST': response = message else: response = scope await send({ 'type': 'http.response.start', 'status': 200, 'headers': [ [b'Content-Type', b'application/json'], ], }) await send({ 'type': 'http.response.body', 'body': json.dumps(response, default=bytes.decode).encode(), }) await send({ 'type': 'http.disconnect', }) return channel ``` ``` > daphne app:app 2018-03-31 22:28:10,823 INFO Starting server at tcp:port=8000:interface=127.0.0.1 2018-03-31 22:28:10,824 INFO HTTP/2 support enabled 2018-03-31 22:28:10,824 INFO Configuring endpoint tcp:port=8000:interface=127.0.0.1 2018-03-31 22:28:10,825 INFO Listening on TCP address 127.0.0.1:8000 127.0.0.1:43436 - - [31/Mar/2018:22:28:17] "GET /" 200 347 127.0.0.1:43440 - - [31/Mar/2018:22:28:22] "POST /" 200 43 127.0.0.1:43446 - - [31/Mar/2018:22:28:42] "POST /" 200 54 ``` ``` > http -b get :8000/ { "type": "http" "http_version": "1.1", "method": "GET", "path": "/", "query_string": "", "root_path": "", "scheme": "http", "headers": [ ["host", "localhost:8000"], ["user-agent", "HTTPie/0.9.9"], ["accept-encoding", "gzip, deflate"], ["accept", "*/*"], ["connection", "keep-alive"] ], "client": ["127.0.0.1", 43360], "server": ["127.0.0.1", 8000], } > http -b -f post :8000/ foo=bar { "body": "foo=bar", "type": "http.request" } > http -b -j post :8000/ foo=bar { "body": "{\"foo\": \"bar\"}", "type": "http.request" } ```

issue_title: Option for removing the New->Terminal menu entry.
issue_body:
There should be an official option for removing the New->Terminal menu entry.

This has been asked many times before, such as in https://github.com/jupyterhub/jupyterhub/issues/1195, but also on StackOverflow and in other forums. The answer always get dismissed with the argument that people can execute code in the notebook anyway, or shell out a terminal command. That is quite valid when the kernel is run on the same host, or in the same container, as the Jupyter Notebook web interface, but when you are running a kernel on a remote host using the ``kernel_gateway`` and ``nb2kg`` packages, or enterprise kernel gateway, the terminal would be created on a different system to where the kernel is running.

In this case of using a kernel gateway, you don't necessarily want people to be able to create the terminal or even see the menu item, because it will run on the system where the Jupyter Notebook web interface is running, not where the kernel is running. You may want to prevent this, leaving the only place a user can run code being where the kernel is running.

The suggestion of uninstalling ``terminado`` package after it has been installed, or setting ``SHELL`` to ``/bin/false`` are both clunky solutions and don't get rid of the menu entry.

Hi, 


in the script viewer (when I open for instance a .py file - _not a notebook_), after typing the string you are looking for in the search-box, you should be able to _cycle among instances until you reach the one you wanted_ , 
and not being able directly to edit after the 2nd time you press the 'return' key - like in other editors.

issue_title: The handler for /kernelspec/name needs to unescape
issue_body:
In `jupyterab/services`, we are url encoding the name of the kernel spec, but the server is not using `utils.url_unescape` on the name, resulting in a 404 if there are escaped characters.

cf https://github.com/jupyterlab/jupyterlab/issues/1018#issuecomment-257007599

issue_title: LBFGS and SaliencyMapAttack are lower-bounded by GradientAttack
issue_body: Right now LBFGS and SaliencyMapAttack use the GradientAttack to initialise the best target class in a misclassification setting. Due to the internal optimisation of the adversarial object it can happen that the best adversarial is actually found by the GradientAttack and not by LBFGS or SaliencyMap. We should reset the adversarial object to avoid this confound. I am currently testing a PR.

issue_title: File Synchronization issue between portal and ddsclient
issue_body:
User feedback:

> As you may be aware, if a project has been modified via the web-portal, ddsclient seems to get confused. For example, If a file from a project has been removed via the web-portal, the ddsclient will ignore an upload request that would re-instantiate that file; it will report "Uploading 0 projects, 0 folders, 1 file.", but no files will be uploaded and the nothing will happen.

Thank you for this amazing library!

I'm using it for modeling classical behaviour of some systems. The idea is to solve complex (4-10 equations) system of ODEs with scipy's ```odeint```, while the right-hand side is evaluated using derivatives of ```hamiltonian``` provided by ```autograd```. Everything works nice for simple systems, but even for middle-sized systems the derivatives of  ```hamiltonian``` are evaluated rather long. Are there any methods to optimize it? (During the solving of ODEs derivatives are being calculated 10.000-50.000 times.)

Sorry for lengthy example, but it seems to be the minimal working bit of code, containing ```hamiltonian``` function.

```
from autograd import grad, grad_named
from autograd.numpy.linalg import inv
import autograd.numpy as np
from itertools import product

class __particle__(object):
	def __init__(self, m, __x__, __y__, __z__,  vars):
		self.m = m
		self.__x__ = __x__
		self.__y__ = __y__
		self.__z__ = __z__

		self.__dx__ = [grad_named(__x__, var) for var in vars]
		self.__dy__ = [grad_named(__y__, var) for var in vars]
		self.__dz__ = [grad_named(__z__, var) for var in vars]

r0 = 1.
particle1 = __particle__(m = 1., __x__ = lambda q: r0 * np.cos(q/2), __y__ = lambda q: 0 * q, __z__ = lambda q: r0 * np.sin(q/2),
	vars = ['q'])
particle2 = __particle__(m = 1., __x__ = lambda q: r0 * np.cos(q/2), __y__ = lambda q: 0 * q, __z__ = lambda q: - r0 * np.sin(q/2),
	vars = ['q'])

particles = [particle1, particle2]

J = 10.
q = np.array([0.1])
p = np.array([0.2])
theta0 = 0.15
varphi0 = 0.01

def hamiltonian(q = None, p = None, theta = None, varphi = None):
	J_vector = np.array([J * np.cos(varphi) * np.sin(theta),
						 J * np.sin(varphi) * np.sin(theta), 
						 J * np.cos(theta)]).reshape((3,))

	Ixx = sum([particle.m * (particle.__y__(*q)**2 + particle.__z__(*q)**2) for particle in particles])
	Iyy = sum([particle.m * (particle.__x__(*q)**2 + particle.__z__(*q)**2) for particle in particles])
	Izz = sum([particle.m * (particle.__x__(*q)**2 + particle.__y__(*q)**2) for particle in particles])
	Ixy = - sum([particle.m * particle.__x__(*q) * particle.__y__(*q) for particle in particles])
	Ixz = - sum([particle.m * particle.__x__(*q) * particle.__z__(*q) for particle in particles])
	Iyz = - sum([particle.m * particle.__y__(*q) * particle.__z__(*q) for particle in particles])
	
	inertia_tensor = np.array([[Ixx, Ixy, Ixz], [Ixy, Iyy, Iyz], [Ixz, Iyz, Izz]])
	inertia_tensor = inertia_tensor.reshape((3, 3))

	a = np.array([]).reshape((1, 0))
	for i, j in product(range(q.shape[0]), range(q.shape[0])):
		res = np.array([sum([particle.m * (particle.__dx__[i](*q) * particle.__dx__[j](*q) + \
								   		   particle.__dy__[i](*q) * particle.__dy__[j](*q) + \
								   		   particle.__dz__[i](*q) * particle.__dz__[j](*q)) 
						for particle in particles])]).reshape((1,1))
		a = np.hstack((a, res))

	a = a.reshape((q.shape[0], q.shape[0]))

 	A = np.array([]).reshape((3,0))
 	for k in range(q.shape[0]):
 		res = np.zeros((3,1))
 		for particle in particles:
 			vec1 = np.array([particle.__x__(*q), particle.__y__(*q), particle.__z__(*q)]).reshape((3,))
 			vec2 = np.array([particle.__dx__[k](*q), particle.__dy__[k](*q), particle.__dz__[k](*q)]).reshape((3,))
 			res = np.hstack((res, np.array([particle.m * np.cross(vec1, vec2)]).reshape((3, 1))))

 		A = np.hstack((A, res.sum(axis = 1).reshape((3,1))))

 	A = A.reshape((3, q.shape[0]))

	G11 = inv(inertia_tensor - np.dot(np.dot(A, inv(a)), A.transpose()))
	G22 = inv(a - np.dot(np.dot(A.transpose(), inv(inertia_tensor)), A))
	G12 = - np.dot(np.dot(G11, A), inv(a))

	angular_component = 0.5 * np.dot(np.dot(J_vector, G11), J_vector)
	kinetic_component = 0.5 * np.dot(np.dot(p.transpose(), G22), p) 
	coriolis_component = np.dot(np.dot(J_vector, G12), p)

	return angular_component + kinetic_component + coriolis_component

dham_dq = grad(hamiltonian, argnum = 0)
print dham_dq(q, p, theta0, varphi0)
```

issue_title: required packages may not get installed
issue_body: python3-pip only suggests build-essentials, python3-wheel and python3-setuptools. If a reactive charm is deployed into a container that already has python3-pip installed, but not the suggestions, then the other packages do not get installed and wheelhouse setup can fail. Which just happened to me, deploying a reactive subordinate to an existing charm.

issue_title: ClusterManager needs an aggressive teardown of the cluster
issue_body: We need to be able to aggressively teardown a cluster, even if things are in an unknown state. We can at least make some assumptions about what the ClusterManager deploys, and we can make a teardown that will attempt to roll back those changes. Even if a step fails, it will continue trying to teardown across all devices. This is critical for using the ClusterManager as a heat plugin, because otherwise, it will cause the heat stack to get stuck in the database, until to removed. Not sure if this is the default or an opt-in teardown.

issue_title: Cannot remove item from basket if another line is available
issue_body:
Issue with `BasketView` not removing an item

### Steps to replicate

* Add an item to your basket
* Add a different item to your basket
* Set the stock on one of the items to `0`, making it unavailable
* Go to the cart page and attempt to remove the item that is still in stock

### Result

The page bounces back with a message `Your basket couldn't be updated` and the product you attempted to remove is still there.

### Expected behavior

At the very least, the generic message should be replaced with the more specific `'<product>' is no longer available to buy (no stock available). Please adjust your basket to continue`. Ideally, the item you attempted to remove should actually be removed.

### Related

If you try to change the quantity of the in stock item, it bounces back with `Your basket couldn't be updated` and the quantity remains unchanged.

### Notes

It looks like this behavior is because no actions are taken if `BasketLineFormSet` is invalid, even though there could be consistent behavior for a "partially valid" formset. I'm unsure of how `data-behaviors="remove"` works. Would it make more sense for this to just be a plain link? Design decision regarding plain link vs javascript?

I have started recently to work with folium.

Has folium any way to set a callback function or just a popup when the user clicks on the polygon area?

I saw some issues maybe about that .. but I'm not sure if this functionality is implemented or not .

thanks for your attention
my best regards

```python
import pandas as gpd
import folium
from folium.plugins import HeatMap

# loading the shapefile and adding it to the folium map
shp = gpd.read_file('./shapefiles/fortal118.shp')
shp.crs = "+proj=utm +zone=24 +ellps=WGS84 +datum=WGS84 +units=m +no_defs +south"
shp = shp.to_crs("+proj=longlat +ellps=WGS84 +datum=WGS84 +no_defs")

Map = folium.Map(location=[-3.731862, -38.526669], zoom_start=12, tiles='cartodbpositron')
folium.GeoJson(shp).add_to(Map)

# loading the data to use in order to make the heatmap
cases2011 = pd.read_csv('cases2011.dat', sep=" ", names=['day', 'month', 'lon', 'lat', 'label'], usecols=['lon', 'lat'])
cases2011['amount'] = np.random.randint(0, 100, size=len(cases2011))

# constructing the heatmap and adding it to the folium map
max_amount = float(cases2011['amount'].max())
hm_wide = HeatMap(zip(cases2011.lat.values, cases2011.lon.values, cases2011.amount.values),
                 min_opacity=0.1,
                 max_val=max_amount,
                 radius=17, blur=15,
                 max_zoom=10)
Map.add_child(hm_wide)
# showing the figure
Map
```
#### Problem description

The issue is that it was supposed to show a figure with the folium map, the shapefile and the heatmap generated. The heatmap is not displayed, just the folium map and the shapefile appears.

#### Expected Output
 
The expected output should be similar to the figure of this post tutorial https://alcidanalytics.com/p/geographic-heatmap-in-python


#### Output of ``folium.__version__``

0.5.0


Could anyone help me with this issue, please?

I would like my map to be shown inside NBviewer just as in the case of [this example](http://nbviewer.ipython.org/github/urschrei/Geopython/blob/master/convert_folium.ipynb) (`Out[18]`) or [that one](http://nbviewer.ipython.org/gist/bburky/7763555/folium-ipython.ipynb) (`Out[4]`) , but [getting a no-color map](http://nbviewer.ipython.org/github/oztalha/GeoInfluence/blob/master/analysis/Desc%20Stats%20of%20the%20Challengers.ipynb?flush_cache=true) (`Out[4]`) or a 404 error instead (`Out[36]`). What might be causing mine to fail, my code is identical to the working examples.

Note: Unlike nbviewer, I can see the map correctly inside my local IPYNB viewer:

issue_title: Windows thread interrupt will not sample GIL released threads
issue_body: Currently, the setup will always sample the thread holding the GIL. This means if a thread releases the GIL to e.g. compress data, it will not be sampled (even though a significant amount of time is spent in compression)

It would be a beautiful thing for B2 CLT to display the upload speed and progress, similar to what wget does.

This kind of feedback would also give assurance that something _is_ happening.

issue_title: AttributeError exception when attempting to deserialize None
issue_body:
When attempting to deserialize None into a Schema, an AttributeError exception is thrown.

```python
from marshmallow import Schema


class MySchema(Schema):
    class Meta:
        fields = ('field1', 'field2')


test_dict = {'field1': 'value 1',
             'field2': 'value 2',
             'something': 'hey'}

assert MySchema().load(test_dict).data == {k: test_dict[k] for k in test_dict
                                           if k is not 'something'}
assert MySchema().load({}).data == {}
assert MySchema().load(None).data is None

```

The final assert throws . . . 

```sh
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "test.py", line 16, in <module>
    assert MySchema().load(None).data is None
  File "/Users/bjones/Desktop/marshmallow/lib/python2.7/site-packages/marshmallow/schema.py", line 490, in load
    result, errors = self._do_load(data, many, postprocess=True)
  File "/Users/bjones/Desktop/marshmallow/lib/python2.7/site-packages/marshmallow/schema.py", line 598, in _do_load
    dict_class=self.dict_class
  File "/Users/bjones/Desktop/marshmallow/lib/python2.7/site-packages/marshmallow/fields.py", line 277, in deserialize
    raw_value = data.get(attr_name, missing)
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'get'
```

This is with 1.2.0.

Obvously the test case is trivial to work around, but in practice I am trying to deserialize a much larger collection where some of the members may be None.

Hello
I managed to install geonode 2.8rc11 + cartoview 1.8.1 + geoserver 2.12 on Ubuntu 16.04 in a clean installation.
But some Apps were not installed as WebApp Builder or Cartoview TerriaMap Viewer (Incompatible)
What could be the problem?

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7094961/40982244-0a00c352-68dd-11e8-8936-0a71c75d8edb.png)

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
The PVSystem class is big enough and popular enough that it deserves its own prominent documentation page. Existing documentation/explanation is spread across:

* [API documentation](http://pvlib-python.readthedocs.io/en/latest/generated/pvlib.pvsystem.PVSystem.html#pvlib.pvsystem.PVSystem)
* [Package Overview](http://pvlib-python.readthedocs.io/en/latest/package_overview.html#object-oriented-location-pvsystem-modelchain)
* [Intro to ModelChain](http://pvlib-python.readthedocs.io/en/latest/modelchain.html#ModelChain)
* Issues such as #319 #492 
* PVPMC 2016 pvlib users group slides

and probably some more places that I forgot about.


**Describe the solution you'd like**
Add ``pvsystem.rst`` documentation that explains the class and how to use it. Topics include:

* Design philosophy (system attributes are object attributes, things that happen to the system are method arguments, all of that is passed to the base functions)
* ``module_parameters``, ``inverter_parameters``, ``losses_parameters`` containers. Examples from SAM database and user defined dicts
* ``modules_per_string`` and ``strings_per_inverter``
* miscellaneous attributes 
* ``SingleAxisTracker`` is a subclass of ``PVSystem``
* ``LocalizedPVSystem`` -- I would probably skip this on initial implementation because I am still skeptical of its long-term viability.

For ease of writing, it could be a ``pvsystem.ipynb`` file that ``nbsphinx`` converts to ``.rst`` at build time (this is how the ModelChain page is created).


**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Add much more information to the ``PVSystem`` docstring.


**Additional context**
This issue (I hope) addresses the request for improved documentation in #491.

class UserRepo:

Since we have some of our commit guidance buried away in a guide, we should [create a PR template that GitHub can pick up](https://github.com/blog/2111-issue-and-pull-request-templates). I'm guessing we could make it similar to redux-observable's:

```
<!-- If this is your first PR for nteract, please mark these boxes to confirm (otherwise you can exclude them)-->

issue_title: Stopping music stream rather than using 'q' command
issue_body:
Are there any commands or ways to stop multiple threads of mpsyt sound-only-stream running at once?

I'm using mpsyt on RasPi(Raspbian) as a music streaming player in our office. And works great.

I know, from SSH, using 'q' command or 'ctl+c' will end mpsyt run, and stop the current music playing.

But since I'm the only SSH user in my office, I made a simple PHP program that calls .sh file that streams sound-only youtube clip using mpsyt, so anyone could request the music to play.

The problem is, while playing a song, if another one requests a song, it plays on top of each other.
So I want to stop the current play or kill all the streams before playing the new one.

What would be the best way to stop current play, rather than quitting the app (using 'q' cmd)?

I have a few cql scripts and a python script to migrate. The python script is something like this:
```
from cassandra.cluster import Session
from cassandra.query import SimpleStatement, PreparedStatement, dict_factory
import logging

log = logging.getLogger(__name__)


def execute(session: Session):
    session.row_factory = dict_factory

    newtable_schema = """
    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS table(
        id int,
        time bigint,
        PRIMARY KEY (id, time)
    );
    """
    __migrate_table(session, "table", newtable_schema)


def __migrate_table(session: Session, original_table: str, new_schema: str):
    log.info(f"Migrating table {original_table} to a new schema {new_schema}")
    tmp_table = f"tmp_{original_table}"
    tmp_create = new_schema.replace(original_table, tmp_table)
    session.execute(tmp_create)

    __copy(session, original_table, tmp_table)

    session.execute(f"DROP TABLE IF EXISTS {original_table}")
    session.execute(new_schema)

    __copy(session, tmp_table, original_table)

    session.execute(f"DROP TABLE {tmp_table}")


def __copy(session: Session, from_table: str, to_table: str, batch_size: int = 10000):
    log.info(f"Copying table {from_table} to {to_table} started")
    select_statement = SimpleStatement(f"SELECT * FROM {from_table}", fetch_size=batch_size)
    query_result = session.execute(select_statement)

    table_columns = query_result.column_names
    column_string = ", ".join(table_columns)
    interpolation_string = ", ".join(["?"] * len(table_columns))
    insert_statement = session.prepare(f"INSERT INTO {to_table} ({column_string}) VALUES ({interpolation_string})")

    cnt = 0
    for row in query_result:
        session.execute(insert_statement, row)
        cnt += 1
        if cnt % batch_size == 0:
            log.info(f"{batch_size} rows copied")
    log.info(f"Copying table {from_table} to {to_table} finished")

```

issue_title: Empty js files colliding with django/contrib/admin
issue_body:
Why does this [folder `jet/static/admin/js/admin`](https://github.com/geex-arts/django-jet/tree/master/jet/static/admin/js/admin) exist if both files serve up empty documents. This only stirs up problems for collectstatic when `django/contrib/admin/static/admin/js/admin` is collected. The actual files which are being contained `DateTimeShortcuts.js` and `RelatedObjectLookups.js` don't get copied because `It will be ignored since only the first encountered file is collected. If this is not what you want, make sure every static file has a unique path.` and then the only assets collected are the empty ones. 

Is this intentional for some reason because if only the empty ones get served, multiselect fields like the one [here](http://demo.jet.geex-arts.com/admin/auth/user/1/change/#/tab/module_2/) under User Permissions don't work. I am forced to delete the empty ones manually so the ones in contrib are served.
```
Django=v1.9
Django JET=v1.0.6
Python=3.6
```

issue_title: Meaning of published depth values in ROS
issue_body:
* Before opening a new issue, we wanted to provide you with some useful suggestions (Click "Preview" above for a better view):

    * Consider checking out SDK [examples](https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/tree/master/examples#sample-code-for-intel-realsense-cameras).
    * Have you looked in our [documentations](https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/tree/master/doc#useful-links)?
    * Is you question a [frequently asked one](https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/wiki/Troubleshooting-Q%26A)?
    * Try [searching our GitHub Issues](https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aissue) (open and closed) for a similar issue.

* All users are welcomed to report bugs, ask questions, suggest or request enhancements and generally feel free to open new issue, even if they haven't followed any of the suggestions above :)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Required Info                         |                                                                |
|---------------------------------|------------------------------------------- |
| Camera Model                       | D436 | 
| Operating System & Version |   Ubuntu 14 | 
| Platform                                 | PC |
| Language                            |  ROS                       | 

### Issue Description
I am using the realsense2_camera launch files and I am publishing the /camera/aligned_depth_to_color/image_raw channel.
Teh depth values are given in Z16 format from [0,64). I am wondering what the actual resolution is since I cannot find that anywhere. When using the pyrealsense2 library in Python, I can query the depth scale to convert the Z16 values to meaningful metric values. Is it safe to assume the same scale is used for the values published in ROS?

issue_title: Feature request: file references relative to top-level srcdir/builddir
issue_body:
At the moment it is necessary to write

```
 "git_repo": "../..",
...
    "c_sources": [
        "../../src/foo/*.c", 
```

It would be great if there was a way to treat all the `c_sources`, etc., as relative to the top level, or a way to specifically say "at top level" like Automake's `$(top_srcdir)`.

If documenting generated code, it might also be necessary to allow a mixture of files in the ${srcdir} and the ${builddir}. This would be most easily done by having Hotdoc's getcwd() be the ${builddir} (to which everything else is relative), and having the Automake glue pass command-line arguments `--top-srcdir=${top_srcdir} --top-builddir=${top_builddir}` or something.

Straw-man syntax:

```
 "search_path": ["${top_srcdir}", "${top_builddir}"],
...
    "c_sources": [
        "src/foo/*.c", /* really ${top_srcdir}/src/foo/whatever.c */
        "src/foo/generated/*.c", /* really ${top_builddir}/src/foo/generated/whatever-gdbus-skeleton.c */
```
Just wondering if it would be feasible to implement the possibility of using the white/black reverse print mode as an option in the 'set' method?

I have:
- [x] searched open and closed issues for duplicates

### Bug description
I've install both the development version cloning the repo and the stable one (in different enviroments). I'm using an epson stylus c63. The printer is correctly detected o both oses, and I've also got some info from the printer (paper status for example).
I've tested own code, some examples of the "examples" directory and the example of the README.md. No error is outputed, only unresponsivness from the printer. 
Since there is no error, is hard to know where is the problem. And since de printer is correctly detected by the library, is hard to know if it is actually supported (according to the ESC/P manual, it is...)

As outlined at https://github.com/pimutils/vdirsyncer/issues/574#issuecomment-284148130, vdirsyncer's default useragent does not conform to the relevant RFCs. We should include the version number like this:

    vdirsyncer/0.15

The new HTTP RFC specifies the syntax [here](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-5.5.3)

issue_title: Implement a Lexicon-based DNS authenticator for Memset
issue_body: Implement an Authenticator plugin which can fulfill a dns-01 challenge using Lexicon.

Currently we always enable OCSP stapling and do so when deploying the certificate. There's an enhancements system in Certbot which can be used to request/enable OCSP stapling, but the Nginx plugin ignores that entirely.

In the short term (for 0.9.0), I think we should remove OSCP stapling from the Nginx plugin. In the long term, we should make sure it properly uses the enhancements system and either:
* Make OCSP stapling the default in Certbot
* Only enable OCSP stapling in Nginx when requested by the user

Currently the nginx plugin adds:
`listen 443 ssl; # managed by Certbot`

It should add the following for IPv6 support:

```
listen 443 ssl; # managed by Certbot
listen [::]:443 ssl; # managed by Certbot
```

~An option for HTTP 2 would be nice too, either by specifying `--http2` flag or just check nginx for compatibility.;~

```
listen 443 ssl http2;
listen [::]:443 ssl http2;
```

issue_title: nginx plugin not properly handling directives inherited from outside of the server block
issue_body:
Reported by @src386 [here](https://github.com/certbot/certbot/issues/4132#issuecomment-320610552):

In an nginx config, if you specify `server_names_hash_bucket_size` outside of a `server` block, we'll fail the challenge. This is because we only search for the directive in the specific server block rather than checking if it was inherited from elsewhere in the config.

While we could do something like removing the directive if we fail to restart nginx after adding it and trying again, the correct solution would be a dramatic change in our parser to be able to properly handle inherited directives.

issue_title: Support registering methods also in KwikAPI
Bug: dialect parameter has no effect when using %%sql --module ..
issue_body:
[An issue was reported on StackOverflow](http://stackoverflow.com/questions/41944649/datalab-how-to-export-big-query-standard-sql-query-to-dataframe) related to the dialect parameter not being honored when using `%%sql --module ...`. This feels like a bug.

The `-d standard` which is used to set the BigQuery dialect to Standard SQL has no effect in the following example:
``
%%sql --module data_name -d standard
``

Currently users still need to specify the dialect parameter when calling `bq.Query.to_dataframe()` which could be confusing.
```
bq.Query(data_name).to_dataframe(dialect='standard')
```

The following works as expected (i.e. when `--module` is not used in `%%sql`)
``
%%sql -d standard
<sql query>
``

issue_title: Resume sending protocols message if a node is back at reachable state
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

The protocol implementation assumes that after [5 seconds](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/b70cd3d5074ed4605b3a643834b74dc5c5604ed7/raiden/network/protocol.py#L66-L67) the partner node is unreachable and drop the message, since Raiden doesn't have messages to request a re-sync this will force the nodes to settle on-chain (given that there is a difference in the internal node states)

## Solution

Instead of dropping the messages the protocol must keep all the unacknowledged messages and resume sending them if the node restores the reachable state, given that the corresponding channel is not closed.

## Related issues

Introduce network state for partner nodes #447
Randomly health check unreachable nodes #448

## Tasklist

- [ ] Change the [greenlet task that sends protocol messages](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/b70cd3d5074ed4605b3a643834b74dc5c5604ed7/raiden/network/protocol.py#L114)
   - [ ] Integrate the send task with the health check (note:  #448 and #247)
   - [ ] Hold the unacknowledged messages (instead of dropping them)
   - [ ] Resume the message queue once the partner is reachable and if the the channel is **not** closed, this **must** be *per* channel

issue_title: Collapse multiple raiden messages within a single transport packet.
issue_body: Raiden messages are ordered using a per target queue, this allows to collapse multiple raiden messages into a single transport packet.

## Problem Definition

With the Byzantium HF looming the testnet urgently needs to be upgraded to geth 1.7.0

## Solution

Upgrade testnet to geth 1.7.0

## Tasklist

- [x] Upgrade testnet to geth 1.7.0

Currently we get the gas price from the ethereum node by calling the `eth_gasPrice` function.

`transforms.Lambda` appears to fail when a function that is passed to it is a class function:

```python
import torchvision

class MyClass(object):

    def my_transform(self, img):
        return img

def my_transform(img):
    return img

t = MyClass()

print('Pure function works')
torchvision.transforms.Lambda(my_transform)
print('Class function fails')
torchvision.transforms.Lambda(t.my_transform)
```
Output:
```
Pure function works
Class function fails
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "test_pytorch.py", line 17, in <module>
    torchvision.transforms.Lambda(t.my_transform)
  File "/braintree/home/qbilius/libs/miniconda3/lib/python3.6/site-packages/torchvision/transforms/transforms.py", line 279, in __init__
    assert isinstance(lambd, types.LambdaType)
AssertionError
```

My understanding it that `isinstance(lambd, types.LambdaType)` is not the best way to check if something is a function or not. Rather, `callable` or `__call__` [should be used](https://stackoverflow.com/a/624939).

I could submit a quick PR if that seems like a good solution.

issue_title: Type errror in _encode_hybi when using the --heartbeat parameter
issue_body:
When using the `--heartbeat` option, every N seconds, websockify gives us a nice `TypeError: can't concat bytes to str` because the value sent from `send_ping()` is a `str` and not a `bytes`.

```
  File "/opt/websockify/websockify/websocketproxy.py", line 180, in do_proxy
    self.send_ping()
  File "/opt/websockify/websockify/websockifyserver.py", line 211, in send_ping
    self.request.ping(data)
  File "/opt/websockify/websockify/websocket.py", line 447, in ping
    self._sendmsg(0x9, data)
  File "/opt/websockify/websockify/websocket.py", line 688, in _sendmsg
    frame = self._encode_hybi(opcode, msg)
  File "/opt/websockify/websockify/websocket.py", line 767, in _encode_hybi
    return header + buf
TypeError: can't concat bytes to str
```

Here's an example of fix. It might need to be applied to pong too.

```diff
diff --git a/websockify/websocketproxy.py b/websockify/websocketproxy.py
index 6c17c10..febf7b3 100644
--- a/websockify/websocketproxy.py
+++ b/websockify/websocketproxy.py
@@ -177,7 +177,7 @@ Traffic Legend:
                 now = time.time()
                 if now > self.heartbeat:
                     self.heartbeat = now + self.server.heartbeat
-                    self.send_ping()
+                    self.send_ping("".encode())
 
             if tqueue: wlist.append(target)
             if cqueue or c_pend: wlist.append(self.request)

```

It happened in this function `image_preprocess`:

```python
def image_preprocess(image, bgr=True):
    with tf.name_scope('image_preprocess'):
        if image.dtype.base_dtype != tf.float32:
            image = tf.cast(image, tf.float32)

        mean = cfg.PREPROC.PIXEL_MEAN
        std = cfg.PREPROC.PIXEL_STD
        if bgr:
            mean = mean[::-1]
            std = std[::-1]
        image_mean = tf.constant(mean, dtype=tf.float32) 
        image_invstd = tf.constant(1.0 / std, dtype=tf.float32) # Here
        image = (image - image_mean) * image_invstd
        return image
```

Thank you!

Hi, I'm wonder what is the weight tensor defined in OptimizedRNN operator:

```
weights
All parameters of the stack packed together in the opaque tensor. The size must be compatible with input attributes passed to the op.
The layout format is the one used by CuDNN and very similar to TensorRT:

The weight structure holds weights and biases for each layer of the network. Each parameter matrix is linearly appended after the previous parameter matrix without padding.

The order of matrixes {K, L, D, R, N, C} is defined as:

K - type of the matrix: weight (first) or bias second
L - The number of layers in the RNN - num_layers
D - The direction of the layer: normal (first) or reverse (second). (in case of directions=2)
R - The type of the connection: input-hidden (first) or hidden-hidden (second)
N - The number of gates matrices in the RNN, dependent on the cell_type: -- For relu or tanh there is one gate -- For gru there are 3 gates ordered as reset, update, hidden -- For lstm there are 4 gates ordered as input, forget, cell, output
C - The size of each matrix, which varies. -- If the linear layer on the input is skipped (skip_input_transform=1) and then for the first layer (L=1) the weight matrix (K=weight) on the input connection (R=input-hidden) is skipped, i.e. has 0 parameters in the list -- For the first layer (L=1) weight matrix (K=weight) on input connection (R=input-hidden), dimensions are {hidden_size, input_size} -- For other layers (L>1) weight matrix (K=weight) on input connection (R=input-hidden), dimensions are {hidden_size, directions * hidden_size} -- For weight matrix (K=weight) on recurrent connection (R=hidden-hidden), dimensions are {hidden_size, hidden_size} -- For all biases (K=bias), dimensions are {hidden_size}
```

When using `{K, L, D, R, N, C}` to denote the order of matrixes, are you describing the order of dimensions within the weight tensor? Is the weight tensor a 6D tensor with each dimension explained as above?
